heading: Pyth-2 stable program/table layer built and page-110 leftovers attacked
text:
type: observation
tags: [qa, pyth2, validated-tables, validated-programs, corpus-ingestion]
body: Built the first stable Pyth-2 program/table layer from the validated workset. `pyth2_validated_programs.py` emitted `pyth2_program_artifacts.json` with the fixed-`e=60` admissible b-set, reflection pairs about 30, and the divisor-5 Fibonacci periodicity rule. `build_pyth2_validated_tables.py` emitted `pyth2_validated_tables.json` with 3 stable item rows, 16 admissibility rows, 8 reflection-pair rows, and 4 Fibonacci periodicity rows. Then attacked the remaining page-110 OCR/manual items with `build_pyth2_page110_unresolved_batch.py`, producing `pyth2_page110_unresolved_reinterpretation_batch_001.json` for 5 leftover items. Current page-110 unresolved split is 2 clean reinterpretations (`every third` for even Fibonacci terms and the bounded coprime bead-pair counts 15/13/35) and 3 flagged source-layer ambiguities (`every fourth` for the source term `3-tri`, `every sixth` for `4-par`, and the e-d count 18 which requires explicit inclusion of the unity root pair (1,1)). This keeps the stable Pyth-2 table/program lane separate from the remaining source-convention cleanup before returning to the noisier page-75 material.
